signature.instructions:
Translate Minecraft modpack / vanilla text entries from source_lang to target_lang.

GENERAL HARD RULES (unchanged):
- Source text contains protected tokens like {{COLOR}}, {{RESET}}, {{ARG}}, {{VAR}}, {{TAG}}, {{BR}}.
  The translation MUST contain every token exactly as many times as the source does — never invent,
  drop, duplicate, or alter a token.
  - Numbered variants ({{COLOR1}} vs {{COLOR2}}, {{ARG}} appearing multiple times, etc.) are distinct
    placeholders — copy each occurrence exactly as written, but you MAY reorder them if the target
    language's grammar requires a different word order than English (this is expected and encouraged
    for natural phrasing — see "argument reordering" below).
  - {{COLOR}} opens a color/format span, the next {{RESET}} closes it — after reordering words, the
    span must still wrap the same words it wrapped in the source. If the source opens a span without
    closing it, do NOT add a closing token yourself.
  - {{ARG}} is a runtime value slot (number/item/player name) — move it wherever it reads naturally
    in the target language, even if that changes its position relative to other {{ARG}} tokens.
  - {{VAR}} and {{TAG}} are verbatim markup — keep them attached to the text they mark.
  - {{BR}} is a line break — keep line structure.
- The glossary (if provided) is binding: when a glossary term appears in the source, its mapped
  target term MUST be used verbatim.
- Return the same keys as the input; translate values only.
- Never leave a value untranslated unless it is a proper noun / credit line / specific fixed-label
  entry that must stay as-is (see "proper nouns" and "known non-translated entries" below).

CRITICAL — OFFICIAL MINECRAFT TERMINOLOGY REQUIREMENT:
Most entries come directly from vanilla Minecraft language files (keys like `commands.*`,
`item.minecraft.*`, `block.minecraft.*`, `key.hotbar.*`, `itemGroup.*`, `jukebox_song.*`,
`subtitles.*`, `painting.minecraft.*`, etc.). For these keys you must reproduce Mojang's OFFICIAL
shipped localization for target_lang as closely as possible — this is NOT a free/literal translation
task. Prioritize matching the real, shipped in-game wording, phrasing, sentence structure, and word
order over a literal word-for-word rendering of the English. Recall of the canonical official string
matters far more than "technically correct but freely phrased" output. Do NOT derive a translation
purely from the literal English semantics — many vanilla names (blocks, banner patterns, keybinds)
have official renderings that diverge substantially from a literal translation. If unsure, recall
harder from memory of the actual shipped strings rather than guessing/deriving.

KEY-TYPE-SPECIFIC PATTERNS (ko_kr — apply the equivalent convention for other languages too):

1. `subtitles.*` entries (accessibility subtitle text) do NOT use the polite verb ending
   (-습니다/-ㅂ니다). Official Korean subtitles use a terse NOUN-FORM / DECLARATIVE-STEM ending
   (명사형 종결 or 동사 어간 종결), e.g.:
   - "Squid dies" → '오징어가 죽음' (NOT '오징어가 죽습니다')
   - "Squid hurts" → '오징어가 상처를 입음' (NOT '오징어가 다칩니다')
   - "Tropical Fish flops" → '열대어가 퍼덕거림' (NOT '열대어가 꼬꾸라집니다')
   - "Trapdoor creaks" → '다락문이 삐걱거림' (trapdoor = 다락문, NOT 덫문)
   - "Enchanted Book taken" → '마법이 부여된 책을 빼냄' (enchanted = 마법이 부여된, taken = 빼냄)
   - "Bee buzzes happily" → '꿀벌이 기쁜 듯 윙윙거림' (bee = 꿀벌, NOT 벌; happily → 기쁜 듯)
   Always end subtitle translations in this short "-음/-함/-됨" style, never "-습니다".

2. `item.minecraft.*` / `block.minecraft.*` entries must match the exact shipped item/block name,
   which is often NOT a literal translation. Verified corrections (do not deviate):
   - "Charcoal" → 숯 (NOT 목탄)
   - "Smithing Template" (e.g. coast_armor_trim_smithing_template) → 대장장이 형판 (NOT 단조 템플릿)
   - "Minecart with Chest" → 상자가 실린 광산 수레 (minecart = 광산 수레, NOT 마인카트; "with X" pattern
     is rendered as "X가 실린 광산 수레")
   - "Wool" → 양털 (NOT 양모) — e.g. "Cyan Wool" → 하늘색/청록색 양털
   - "Slab" → 반블록 (NOT 슬랩/슬래브) — e.g. "Brick Slab" → 벽돌 반블록
   - "Blackstone" → 흑암 (NOT 검은 돌)
   - "End Portal Frame" → 엔드 차원문 틀 (NOT 종말의 포탈 프레임) — "End" → 엔드 (not 종말), "Portal" →
     차원문, "Frame" → 틀
   - "Trapdoor" → 다락문
   - "Advancement" → 발전 과제 (NOT 업적)
   - "Spawn Egg" → 생성 알 (NOT 스폰 알)
   - "Hotbar" → 단축 바 (NOT 핫바)
   - "substring" → 부분 문자열 (NOT 서브스트링)
   - "Minecart" → 광산 수레 (NOT 마인카트)
   When translating any item/block name not listed above, first recall the actual official Korean
   term from memory rather than transliterating or translating word-by-word — many official names
   are non-literal.

3. `block.minecraft.banner.*` entries (banner pattern names) use fixed heraldry-derived Korean
   terminology (e.g. 사각형/십자/줄무늬/갈매기무늬 combined with position words like 왼쪽 위, 오른쪽 위,
   가운데, 위, 아래). WARNING: the position implied by the English pattern-id (e.g. "top_right",
   "left") does NOT always map directly to the same left/right side in the official Korean name —
   heraldic dexter/sinister conventions can cause an apparent mirroring (e.g. the pattern id
   "square_top_right" for the color orange is officially '주황색 왼쪽 위 사각형', not "오른쪽 위"/
   "수직 기둥"). Do not guess a geometric description from the English words — recall the actual
   shipped Korean banner-pattern name as precisely as possible. When genuinely uncertain of the
   exact official string, still produce a plausible heraldry-style Korean phrase (color + position +
   shape noun) rather than an invented geometric term.

4. Command-result sentences should follow the natural Korean grammatical order used by Mojang
   (e.g. "X에게 Y 발전 과제를 승인했습니다" rather than a literal "Y가 X에게 부여되었습니다" construction).
   When a source string has multiple {{ARG}} tokens, expect that the official translation often
   reorders them (e.g. the 2nd English argument may appear before the 1st in Korean) — reorder
   freely to match natural target-language grammar as long as every {{ARG}} occurrence count is
   preserved.

ARGUMENT REORDERING GUIDANCE:
Do not assume {{ARG}} tokens must stay in source order. Read the whole sentence, decide the most
natural target-language word order, then place the tokens accordingly, while keeping the exact same
count of each token type as the source.

PROPER NOUNS / DO-NOT-TRANSLATE CASES:
- Jukebox song entries (`jukebox_song.minecraft.*`) follow the pattern "Composer Name - Song Title"
  (e.g. "C418 - wait", "Samuel Åberg - 5"). These are artist/track credits and must be copied
  through UNCHANGED (verbatim).
- Painting credit entries (`painting.minecraft.*.author`, e.g. "Kristoffer Zetterstrand") are
  real-world artist names and must be copied through UNCHANGED (verbatim) — do NOT transliterate
  or render them in Hangul/target-script. Any key ending in `.author`, or any value that is clearly
  a real person's credited name, must be returned exactly as given in the source.
- Some fixed keybind/demo-mode labels referencing numbered preset content (e.g.
  `key.keyboard.world.1` = "World 1", used for Minecraft's Demo mode world slots) are left
  UNTRANSLATED in the official localization — return such values exactly as in the source (e.g.
  "World 1" stays "World 1", not translated to "월드 1"). If a key/value pattern looks like this kind
  of fixed numbered demo/preset label, prefer keeping it verbatim.
- Other proper nouns (player names, mod/brand names, etc.) that the glossary explicitly says to keep
  must also remain untranslated.

STYLE PER LANGUAGE:
- Korean (ko_kr): natural gamer-facing tone, matching official Mojang Korean localization; never
  append the English original in parentheses/brackets; no romanization of items that have
  established Korean names; prefer official terms over transliterated loanwords (e.g. 생성 알 over
  스폰 알, 단축 바 over 핫바, 광산 수레 over 마인카트, 숯 over 목탄, 양털 over 양모, 반블록 over 슬랩,
  흑암 over 검은 돌). Subtitle strings use the terse noun/stem-ending style described above, never
  polite "-습니다" verb endings.
- Japanese (ja_jp): plain polite register for UI text, matching official Mojang Japanese wording
  where the key is a known vanilla string.
- Chinese (zh_cn / zh_tw): follow official Minecraft terminology of the respective variant exactly.

QUALITY BAR:
Your output will be scored against the real official translation for vanilla keys, primarily on
string similarity (chrF) to that exact official string. A grammatically correct but freely-phrased
translation that diverges from the official wording — including using the wrong grammatical register
(e.g. polite verb endings in subtitles), non-canonical item/block names, mistranslated proper nouns
(e.g. transliterating an artist's name), incorrectly translating fixed English labels that Mojang
leaves untranslated, or literal-but-wrong terminology — will be scored low even if technically
accurate. Always favor recall of the established/canonical Minecraft phrasing (exact wording, ending
style, and terminology) over creative or literal re-translation. Before finalizing each vanilla-key
entry, pause and ask: "Is this a name/term/credit that Mojang's real shipped localization renders
differently from a literal translation, or leaves untranslated entirely?" — if so, use that known/
recalled form instead of a literal rendering. For modpack-specific (non-vanilla) strings without a
known official translation, translate naturally and idiomatically while still following the token
and glossary rules above.

fields.description: mod name, content type (quest/item/tooltip/guidebook), file path hint
binding term rules in 'source = target' form; MUST be followed
key -> source text with protected {{KIND}} tokens
exactly the same keys -> translated text

signature.instructions:
Fix a translation that failed programmatic validation.

Address every listed error. Keep the parts of the translation that are
already correct. Every protected token from the source ({{COLOR}} opens
a color span, {{RESET}} closes it, {{ARG}} is a value slot) must appear
in the fix exactly as many times as in the source, copied exactly as
written. The glossary is binding.
